body.title: Wire engram cursor traversal loop (G-ENGRAM-CURSOR1)
body.result: Traversal loop wired: follows next_node via __entry__ trigger, basket-snapshot loop detection, FORKIF→background jobs. 730 tests green.
body.description:
Wire the engram cursor traversal loop in main.py so BRANCHIF actually follows next_node and FORKIF actually spawns background jobs. D292/D293/D294.

Current state (main.py ~line 4225): when execute_node() returns next_node, it only logs ENGRAM_BRANCH to ring then drops it. Same for spawned.

Required changes:
1. After execute_node() returns, if next_node is set: fetch that memory from self.cortex by node ID, then re-run execute_node() on it with trigger=__entry__ and the same basket. Loop until next_node is None or loop detected.
2. Loop detection (D294): maintain visit_log = {node_id: basket_snapshot} per traversal. On each node arrival, snapshot basket (exclude non-serializable keys like _cortex, _salience). If node_id already in visit_log AND snapshot matches last snapshot → break (infinite loop detected, log warning).
3. Basket snapshot helper: shallow copy of basket excluding keys starting with _ (internal refs). Convert to sorted JSON string for comparison.
4. FORKIF spawned nodes (D293): for each node_id in result.spawned, queue as background job (use existing background job mechanism — look at how other bg jobs are dispatched in main.py). Pass basket snapshot (excluding _ keys) as job context.
5. Keep the existing ENGRAM_BRANCH/ENGRAM_FORK ring writes — they are the trail record (D292).
6. Add tests: traversal follows next_node, loop detection fires on repeated visit with same basket, loop continues on basket mutation, fork spawns background job.

Inertia: MEDIUM (main.py). Read the engram dispatch block (~line 4194-4237) before editing. Do not touch anything outside that block.
